description: This figure plots the polarization resistance (R_p) values derived from electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) fittings of surface oxide films on Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels (LDSs) versus immersion time up to 168 h. The graph compares four conditions: S0 (as-hot-rolled, red stars), S950 (fine-grained after 950 °C solution treatment, green circles), S1050 (intermediate grain size after 1050 °C, blue triangles), and S1150 (coarse-grained after 1150 °C, magenta squares). The S950 data display the highest R_p, rising sharply in the first 24 h and stabilizing near 1.2×10^5 Ω·cm²; citation text reports a value of 114.9 kΩ·cm² at 168 h. S1050 maintains intermediate values around 8.0×10^4 Ω·cm², whereas S1150 and S0 show substantially lower resistances, with S1150 reaching only 50.6 kΩ·cm² at 168 h. The markedly higher and faster-growing R_p for S950 indicates that fine grain size accelerates the formation of a dense, stable Al-rich oxide film, providing superior protection against corrosive media compared to coarse-grained microstructures.
